# UC-22.2.20 · NIS2 Management Body Accountability and Governance Evidence

## Description

Loads each expected governance obligation from `nis2_governance_evidence.csv`, enriches it with roster attributes, then left-joins the latest LMS completion epoch per `person_id` and the latest ServiceNow approval epoch per `policy_name`. It computes `days_since` from the newest proof across Splunk, the roster, or the static registry date, classifies rows as MISSING, OVERDUE (>365 days), or DUE_SOON (>270 days), hashes an `evidence_id`, and returns only rows that still need management attention.

## Value

Regulators and plaintiff theories under NIS2 focus on whether directors and officers actually exercised oversight—not only whether a policy PDF exists. This gives the board secretary and CISO a single place to prove who was trained, which cyber policies were approved by whom, and where evidence gaps exceed a one-year tolerance before audits or insurance renewals.

## Implementation

(1) Publish `nis2_governance_evidence.csv` rows for each required artefact (executive_training, policy_approval, risk_acceptance, board_security_briefing) with `person_id` or `policy_name`, baseline `date`, and `responsible_person`. (2) Keep `nis2_management_roster.csv` aligned with HR for `board_member` and `last_training_date`. (3) Ingest LMS completions into `index=training` with `sourcetype=lms:completion` and ServiceNow GRC into `index=itsm` using TA 1928. (4) Schedule the saved search weekly and alert when any `governance_flag` is OVERDUE or MISSING. (5) Archive the result set to `index=audit_evidence` and retain `index=_audit` action=log entries for the schedule.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Splunk Enterprise 9.x or Splunk Cloud; roles with `rest` access to run transforms and scheduled searches.
• Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055) ≥4.0 **or** a HEC token writing JSON LMS events to `index=training` with `sourcetype=lms:completion`.
• Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928) with API credentials stored in `passwords.conf`; enable modular inputs that materialize `snow:sn_grc_policy` and `snow:task` into `index=itsm` (poll every 300 seconds during business hours).
• Lookup files deployed under `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/<your_app>/lookups/`: `nis2_governance_evidence.csv`, `nis2_management_roster.csv`, optional `nis2_exception_register.csv`, `nis2_person_alias.csv`, `nis2_policy_alias.csv` — register each in `transforms.conf`.
• Indexes: `training`, `itsm`, `_audit`, `audit_evidence`; confirm retention on `audit_evidence` meets legal hold (commonly 2555 days).
• RBAC: governance readers need `search` on those indexes but not `delete`; limit `audit_evidence` to `compliance_analyst` and `board_secretariat` roles.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
• Map LMS extracts so each completion carries stable `person_id` (prefer workforce ID over email), `completion_date`, `course_code`, and `role`. For Azure AD–fed LMS, use TA 4055 inputs: Settings > Data inputs > Office 365 Management > Audit logs, and route derived training events via a `props.conf` `SEDCMD` or `EVAL` to `sourcetype=lms:completion`.
• ServiceNow: in the TA UI, validate table pulls for `sn_grc_policy` (or equivalent) and approval tasks; ensure fields `policy_name`, `approval_status`, `approved_by`, `approval_date`, `review_cycle`, `next_review_date` appear in Splunk JSON.
• Build `nis2_governance_evidence.csv` with one row per legislative artefact (board briefing, cyber policy approval, executive phishing course, risk acceptance). Columns minimally: `evidence_type`, `date`, `responsible_person`, `person_id`, `policy_name`, `risk_id`.
• Run `splunk btool transforms list --debug | findstr nis2_` on Windows or `grep nis2_` on Linux to confirm lookups reload after edits.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Paste the SPL from the `spl` field into Search > New Search, verify fields, then Save As > Report > Schedule `every Sunday 05:00` with time range `Last 800 days` on subsearches and `All time` on the lookup portion.
Understanding this SPL — NIS2 Art.20 ties personal accountability to demonstrable meetings, approvals, and training. The pipeline starts from the **registry** (`nis2_governance_evidence.csv`) so missing CSV rows are as visible as missing telemetry. `lookup nis2_management_roster.csv` adds `board_member` to distinguish directors from executives. Each `join` is intentionally left outer: absence of LMS or ServiceNow rows surfaces as `MISSING` rather than silently looking green. `coalesce` prefers freshest indexed proof, then roster fallbacks, then static `date` for manually attested artefacts. The `where` clause keeps only actionable governance debts for paging; dashboards can comment the clause out to show all green rows.
Schedule action: enable Summary Indexing or `collect` to `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:governance` including `evidence_id`. Mirror dispatcher proof with `index=_audit` action=edit:search (default 30 days is enough for run verification).

Step 3 — Validate
• **Count parity**: `| inputlookup nis2_governance_evidence.csv | stats count` must equal board-approved inventory entries; compare to PDF registry stored in the board portal.
• **Spot join**: pick `person_id=P-1001`, run `index=training sourcetype=lms:completion person_id=P-1001 | head 1` and confirm `completion_epoch` matches LMS UI.
• **ServiceNow spot**: open the GRC record in the ServiceNow portal and compare `approval_date` to the latest event in `index=itsm sourcetype=snow:sn_grc_policy policy_name="<name>"`.
• **Stale threshold**: temporarily set CSV `date` to 400 days ago for a sandbox row; confirm `governance_flag=OVERDUE`.
• **Audit hook**: search `index=_audit action=search info=completed` for the saved-search name to capture operator ID and duration.

Step 4 — Operationalize
• Add an ES correlation search or standard alert email to `board_secretariat@example.com` when `governance_flag=MISSING`.
• Dashboard layout: Row 1 KPI tiles; Row 2 interactive table; Row 3 evidence-type breakdown; Row 4 timeline (use `timechart` on `indexed_approval_epoch`).
• Quarterly: export CSV from the panel, sign externally, and attach minute IDs from the corporate secretary.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Empty joins**: verify `person_id` formats (leading zeros) between LMS and roster; normalize with `| eval person_id=upper(trim(person_id))` before joining.
• **Approval epoch null**: `approval_date` might be non-ISO; extend `strptime` with `%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S` or use `strptime` multi-pattern via `coalesce`.
• **Too many DUE_SOON alerts**: narrow `course_code` filters in the LMS subsearch with `where course_code IN (...)`.
• **Saved search skipped**: check `index=_internal source=*scheduler*` for `skipped` reason (concurrency); raise `max_concurrent` in `savedsearches.conf` if licensed.
• **Lookup not found**: run `rest /services/data/transforms/lookup` with admin cookie to see `_invalid` rows; fix `permissions` stanza to `global` scope.

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_governance_evidence.csv
| lookup nis2_management_roster.csv person_id OUTPUT board_member, name, role, last_training_date
| eval roster_training_epoch=strptime(last_training_date, "%Y-%m-%d")
| join type=left person_id [
    search index=training sourcetype IN ("lms:completion", "lms:enrollment") earliest=-800d
    | eval completion_epoch=strptime(completion_date, "%Y-%m-%d")
    | stats max(completion_epoch) as indexed_training_epoch by person_id
]
| join type=left policy_name [
    search index=itsm sourcetype IN ("snow:sn_grc_policy", "snow:task") earliest=-800d
    | eval approval_epoch=strptime(approval_date, "%Y-%m-%d")
    | stats max(approval_epoch) as indexed_approval_epoch values(approved_by) as approval_chain by policy_name
]
| eval evidence_epoch=coalesce(indexed_training_epoch, indexed_approval_epoch, roster_training_epoch, strptime(date, "%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval days_since=if(isnotnull(evidence_epoch), round((now()-evidence_epoch)/86400, 0), null())
| eval governance_flag=case(
    isnull(evidence_epoch), "MISSING",
    days_since>365, "OVERDUE",
    days_since>270, "DUE_SOON",
    1=1, "CURRENT")
| eval evidence_id=sha256(evidence_type . "|" . coalesce(person_id, policy_name, responsible_person) . "|" . if(isnotnull(evidence_epoch), tostring(evidence_epoch), "null"))
| where governance_flag IN ("MISSING", "OVERDUE", "DUE_SOON")
| table evidence_id, evidence_type, board_member, name, role, person_id, policy_name, course_code, responsible_person, approval_chain, days_since, governance_flag, indexed_training_epoch, indexed_approval_epoch
| sort - days_since
```

## Visualization

Row 1 — Single value: count of OVERDUE plus MISSING rows. Row 2 — Table sorted by `days_since` with columns `governance_flag`, `name`, `role`, `evidence_type`, `policy_name`, `course_code`, `evidence_id`. Row 3 — Bar chart: counts by `evidence_type` and flag. Row 4 — Timeline: `approval_date` and `completion_date` overlays for drilldown.
